concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are not stored at contiguous memory locations. Each element points to the next element.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit of a linked list, containing data and a reference (pointer) to the next node.

concepts.name: Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A variable that stores the memory address of another variable, used to link nodes in a linked list.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A condition in a linked list where a node's pointer points back to a previously visited node, creating an infinite loop.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: The process of identifying whether a cycle exists within a linked list.

concepts.name: Floyd's Cycle-Finding Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that uses two pointers, one moving at a slow pace and the other at a fast pace, to detect cycles.

concepts.name: Slow Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer in Floyd's algorithm that moves one step at a time through the linked list.

concepts.name: Fast Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer in Floyd's algorithm that moves two steps at a time through the linked list.

concepts.name: Meeting Point
concepts.short_definition: The node where the slow and fast pointers meet if a cycle exists in the linked list.

concepts.name: Cycle Start Node
concepts.short_definition: The first node in the linked list that is part of the cycle.
